title: Ethereum Foundation to Scale Back, Focus on Core Research
summary: The Ethereum Foundation (EF) will reduce its operations and sell less ETH, focusing on core research. Vitalik Buterin emphasized a shift to 'longevity over breadth,' prioritizing Ethereum's CROPS properties.
body:
The Ethereum Foundation (EF) announced plans to downsize and refocus its efforts, selling fewer ETH tokens and concentrating on core research. Vitalik Buterin, co-founder of Ethereum, stated that the foundation will prioritize 'longevity over breadth,' aiming to ensure the long-term sustainability of Ethereum's ecosystem. This shift comes amid reports of key researchers leaving the foundation, prompting a more streamlined approach.

Buterin highlighted that the EF will focus exclusively on Ethereum's CROPS properties—Censorship Resistance, Resilience, Openness, Permissionlessness, and Scalability. This strategic pivot is part of a broader effort to ensure the foundation's resources are used more effectively, with less emphasis on broader initiatives and more on critical research and development.

For everyday users, this means that the Ethereum Foundation will be more selective in its projects, potentially leading to fewer but more impactful updates. The decision to sell less ETH could also stabilize the market, as the foundation's sales have historically influenced ETH's price. Users can expect a more focused and sustainable approach to Ethereum's development, with a stronger emphasis on its core principles.

Moving forward, watch for the EF's specific research initiatives and any further announcements regarding its reduced operational scope. This shift could lead to a more resilient and focused Ethereum ecosystem, benefiting long-term users and developers alike.

This shift to 'longevity over breadth' comes as key researchers leave. The EF will concentrate on censorship resistance, resilience, openness, permissionlessness, and scalability.

What does this mean for you? Fewer but more impactful updates. Watch for EF's research focus—this could stabilize ETH and strengthen Ethereum's future. Read more → https://cryptocatalyst.news/articles/2026-05-24-ethereum-foundation-to-scale-back-focus-on-core-research
